source: Remediation sequence C1-C6 (fresh-eyes audit 2026-06-06); filed for later manual implementation. See docs/PROJECT_MAP.md and the approved plan.
title: Make sprint.py honest: one real end-to-end run, or downgrade the claim (C3)
what_failed: ABOUT.md calls the autonomous sprint runner '✅ Working' but it has never closed a ticket in production (0 sprint branches, 0 auto_runner solutions). Guardrails refuse ~80% of the repo, so nothing gets through.
why_likely: Built as a design, never operationalized on a real ticket class.
migration_plan: 1. Pick ONE narrow safe-component ticket (e.g. a docs/test ticket from this batch) and run sprint.py --auto-implement end-to-end on a branch.
2. If it closes the ticket (branch + auto_runner solution exist), it is honestly 'working' - record the evidence.
3. If it cannot, DOWNGRADE the status to 'designed, not operational' in ABOUT.md and README. No middle '✅'.
4. Debugging: add a one-line sprint run-log (ran / picked / outcome / cost) so 'did it run' is always answerable.
implementation_notes: Smart and simple: prove it once or stop claiming it. The run-log is the debugging aid.
analysis: C3: the 'self-improve' pillar must be true, not aspirational. Either outcome is honest; the current state is not.
